description_long: This object is a long, double-edged dagger or short sword with a straight, tapering blade made of dark metal, likely bronze or iron. The blade is slender and gradually narrows to a sharp point, suitable for thrusting or stabbing. The handle is short, cylindrical, and slightly flared at the pommel, appearing to be made of the same metal as the blade, with no visible wrapping or grip texture. There is no crossguard or hand protection between the blade and handle, giving it a minimalist, utilitarian appearance. The surface of the blade and handle is matte and shows signs of age or patina, suggesting it could be an antique or historical replica. No decorative elements or ornamentation are visible on any part of the object.
description: A straight, double-edged dagger or short sword with a dark, tapered blade and a simple, flared metal handle. The surface is matte and unadorned, with no guard or visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade and the flared pommel handle.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the cylindrical handle profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the blade and handle shape seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again emphasizing the blade's narrow profile and the handle's end.
attribute_handle_design: short, cylindrical, slightly flared pommel, smooth metal
